_meta.description: ID/passport photo specs for the photo maker. Pixel sizes are mm@300DPI derivations; official sources state physical mm + head size, and 300 DPI is the standard print convention (flagged where the source omits DPI).

specs.label: US Passport / Visa — 2×2 in
specs.note: White/off-white background, neutral expression, no glasses. State Dept states size + head height but not DPI; 600×600 is the 300-DPI standard.

specs.label: Schengen / EU Visa — 35×45 mm
specs.note: EU Visa Code + ICAO. White background safest (light grey also accepted). Face fills 70–80% of height.

specs.label: UK Passport — 35×45 mm
specs.note: Plain light-grey or cream background (NOT pure white). Head+shoulders fill 65–75% of frame.

specs.label: Australia Passport — 35×45 mm
specs.note: Neutral or light-grey background contrasting with face. Shadowless, even lighting.

specs.label: India Passport — 35×45 mm
specs.note: Plain white background, large head height (36–38 mm), uniform lighting, no shadows.

specs.label: Canada Passport — 50×70 mm
specs.note: Plain white background, chin-to-crown 31–36 mm. IRCC states mm + head size but not print DPI; 591×827 is the 300-DPI derivation.

printSheet._note: 4×6 in photo paper @ 300 DPI for tiling multiple copies.
